mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS reduces discount-rate pressure on equities and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days after easing begins.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 25 bps over 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average 20d return is not above the unconditional baseline in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and earnings-risk escalation, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as analysts cut profit expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average return is non-negative across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise, consumer demand and payroll income are jointly improving, which supports near-term revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive condition does not outperform each proxy used alone in directional accuracy, or if 5d forward SPX returns are not positive on average when both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO together with higher HOUST indicates improving real activity and capacity utilization, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through stronger earnings breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined industrial-production and housing-strength signal fails to produce positive 60d returns in a majority of instances, or if its out-of-sample Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in GDPC1 growth signals weaker aggregate demand and lower forward earnings growth, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if low-growth GDP quarters are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses directional accuracy for two consecutive reporting cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains subdued, real rates are likely falling, which supports equity valuation expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy triggers are not associated with positive 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term contributes no incremental predictive power versus CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS separately.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend combined with rising UNRATE captures labor-market contraction and weaker household income growth, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-weakness signal does not produce negative 5d returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the average post-signal return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving INDPRO and rising HOUST should lead the equity market by signaling rebuilding output and construction demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive monthly prints and INDPRO is above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if recovery-trigger episodes fail to outperform unconditional 60d returns, or if the signal turns negative in a rolling out-of-sample evaluation over any 18-month window.
